key_insight: A multi-task spatiotemporal deep neural network can use top-view weld pool images plus welding parameters to predict penetration state, depth, and weld seam morphology in real time.
engineering_relevance: The method supports in-situ quality control for laser penetration welding by enabling earlier detection of defective penetration and improved process monitoring.
method: Capture weld pool images with a CMOS camera during welding.
Fuse spatial-temporal features from image sequences with welding parameters using CNNs and state-space models.
Train a multi-task model to jointly predict penetration state, depth, and cross-section morphology.
evidence_signal: On the test set, the model achieved 99.35% penetration-state accuracy, 1.79 mm penetration-depth error, and 95.65% weld cross-section reconstruction accuracy.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality and diversity of the constructed dataset, so generalization to new welding setups or materials should be validated.
paper_title: A multi-task spatiotemporal deep neural network for predicting penetration depth and morphology in laser welding
